concerns.text: The plan explicitly states 'NO verified source found' for Kepler DR25 and KIC, relying on a fallback to 'manual URL verification' or 'local mock'. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and Principle III (Data Hygiene), which mandate that external datasets MUST be fetched from the same canonical source on every run and checksummed. A plan that admits the primary data source is unverified and potentially inaccessible cannot proceed to implementation; the dataset citation must be resolved to a specific, reachable URL (e.g., MAST archive direct link) before the pipeline can be considered feasible.

The 'Assumption about data availability' claims the Kepler DR catalog is accessible via MAST without rate-limiting. However, the plan.md admits the source is unverified. If the specific DR25 table URL is not pinned and verified, the pipeline will fail on CI (which has no internet access for manual verification) or produce non-reproducible results if the MAST structure changes. The plan must cite the exact dataset URL (e.g., https://archive.stsci.edu/kepler/dr25/...) to satisfy the 'Verified Datasets' requirement and ensure the 'Single Source of Truth'.
